title: Bad generic error on podman pull
body:
```
./bin/podman pull docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo
Trying to pull docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo...Failed
error pulling image "docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo": unable to pull docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo: unable to find image on registries defined in /etc/containers/registries.conf, or you do not have pull access
```
I specified a full path and transport to an image, so no registries were used, and yet it gives me an error message that has little to do with what actually happened.  Containers/image returns the correct message, although not awesome

```
# skopeo copy docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo dir:/tmp/dan
FATA[0000] Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://this-does-not-exist.example.com/v2/: dial tcp: lookup this-does-not-exist.example.com on 10.5.30.160:53: no such host 
```

comments.body: @mtrmac Are any of the changes you are doing going to improve this?

> @mtrmac Are any of the changes you are doing going to improve this?

So far I have only simplified the implementation of writing that unhelpful error message :)  It’s not in what I think of as the scope: “clarify, refactor, simplify and fix dealing with image names”, but that scope is of course negotiable (the above-mentioned simplification was not _exactly_ in scope either).

This might also be a good intern project: very small/localized, simple but requires some design thinking, so not mind-numbingly trivial, easy to demo.

---

For the record, https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/849 is the buildah counterpart, and lists some error kinds to consider.
+1 on @mtrmac's thoughts.  In addition https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/881 addresses some of this in Buildah land.

This is what I see now with that patch.
./buildah from docker://this-does-not-exist.example.com/foo
invalid reference format

Might be worse.
@mheon Is there a tracker issue or reference in containers/image for the issue? The state of this issue seems a bit scattered.
@vrothberg Not that I know of. @mtrmac Should we create one?

If there is a bug, sure, file one — but please be more specific than this report, which does not really say what c/image should do differently (the `unable to find image on registries` text comes from `podman` directly, and the bug is mostly that c/image errors are not presented to the user).
Right we need to fix this in one plave and then attempt to use it in buildah and podman.  Whether the fix goes into containers/image or into a subpackage of buildah or podman...
This seems to be fixed now, closing.
